summary.intent: Implement a QrCode agnostic core component (issue #288), get it through Codex review by addressing all findings, and merge the PR with CI green and Codex approval.
summary.outcome: All four Codex review findings were addressed (URL case-insensitivity, role='img' relocated from root to pattern, overlay alt='', module_size sanitization), xci-fast passed all 10 gates, and the fixes were committed and pushed. The PR remains open pending Codex's second-round verdict.

repo: The CI gate is `cargo xci-fast` with 10 checks: fmt, clippy, unit, integration, adapter, spec-compile-snippets, error-variant-coverage, mutual-exclusion, snapshot-count, coverage-native.
Codex bot (`chatgpt-codex-connector[bot]`) auto-triggers on new PRs with a 👀 reaction; a terminal verdict is signaled by 👀 dropping and review threads opening (no 👍).
PRs containing .snap fixture files require the `snapshot-reviewed` label before merging.
The codebase convention for f64 props is to guard with `is_finite()` and `<= 0.0`, falling back to a named default constant (e.g., `DEFAULT_MODULE_SIZE = 4.0`), matching `meter` and `angle_slider`.
Both leptos and dioxus adapter specs must be kept in sync with core component spec changes — each has parts tables, merge tables, and invariant lines that can become stale independently.
`HtmlAttr::Alt` exists in the crate for emitting decorative `alt=""` on overlay images.
Snapshot files are per-test and named by full test path; orphaned snapshots from renamed or removed tests must be deleted manually.

code.finding: role='img' and aria-label were moved from root_attrs to pattern_attrs so that a DownloadTrigger nested under Root stays in the a11y tree (role='img' is a leaf role that prunes children). Root is now a neutral sized container.
URL scheme matching must use case-insensitive comparison (eq_ignore_ascii_case on the scheme) rather than starts_with("https://") because HTTP schemes are case-insensitive per spec.
A stale doc comment on the Part::Root variant still referenced role='img' after the T2 fix — xci-fast did not catch it, requiring a manual post-pass check before committing.
Proptest strategies for qr_code needed arb_module_size broadened to include invalid values (non-finite, non-positive) to cover the new sanitization path.

workflow: Codex review loop pattern: post @codex review trigger → poll for 👀-drop + threads → address all threads with TDD + no coverage regression → push → reply to and resolve each thread → re-trigger @codex review.
Architectural/accessibility decisions that reshape the documented spec contract should be surfaced to the user before implementing (T2 was blocked on user input for role placement).
Spec edits require syncing three documents: the core component spec plus both adapter specs (leptos and dioxus), each with parts tables, merge tables, and invariant prose.
Markdown table editors/formatters auto-reformat on save, so verifying content correctness rather than column alignment is sufficient after an edit.

friction: Codex returned 4 unresolved threads instead of 👍, requiring a full fix-and-re-trigger cycle rather than a straight merge.
T2 (role='img' placement) blocked on a user design decision — the spec's documented anatomy was in conflict with correct a11y semantics, requiring an explicit call on direction.
Adapter specs (leptos and dioxus) each had 3 stale spots referencing the old role='img' placement on Root, discovered only after the core spec was already updated.
A stale `Part::Root` doc comment referencing role='img' slipped through xci-fast and required a manual fix + quick re-verify after the gate had already passed.
Markdown table alignment broke after editing adapter spec rows, requiring re-padding or relying on the formatter to auto-correct.
open_items: Codex second-round review pending — transcript ends after push, before Codex responds to the re-trigger.
PR #703 remains open; merge requires both CI green (likely satisfied) and Codex 👍 (not yet received).
If Codex raises new threads in round 2, another fix-push-resolve-retrigger cycle will be needed.
